page: Microsoft Azure E20_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports hardware-assisted virtualization, AVX-512 vector extensions, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA-NI.

This instance is configured with 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning all storage requirements must be met through attached network volumes. The memory subsystem achieves an average read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1048.05 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write bandwidths reach 112.06 GB/sec and 59.15 GB/sec, respectively.

Benchmark evaluations place the E20_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU score is 1898.05, and the multi-core score is 19418.56. In database operations, the server processes 19.7 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 9953.34 in PassMark database tests. Software compilation with Clang yields a Geekbench score of 18538.0.

While the server demonstrates strong text generation performance of 273.66 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, it exhibits poor performance on larger models, processing only 4.16 tokens/sec for 70B model prompts. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe drives.
description: Microsoft Azure E20_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 10 physical cores. Featuring 160.0 GB of RAM, it provides a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory density, though it lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most tasks, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1623.0 and a multi-core score of 11025.0. It delivers solid database performance, processing 19.7 million Redis SET operations per second, and shows strong text generation speeds of 273.66 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models. However, prompt processing for large 70B LLM models is poor at 4.16 tokens/sec. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and caching layers that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and no local storage, suited for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
160.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Average performance across database, compression, and compilation benchmarks
Strong text generation speed of 273.66 tokens/sec for small LLM models
Suited for in-memory caching, relational databases, and enterprise applications
